creator: gusber@kth.se
text:
I don't trust this workaround completely and i'm afraid you have to work ou=
 t parts of it yourself.=20
 The basic problem is that the symbols the linker is missing is built by bui=
 lding the latest version of samba.=20
 The linker doesn't know that certain directories in the /usr/ports/net/samb=
 a4/work/.... should be included so it fails.=20
 
 What i did was:
 0. locate the libraries within the /usr/ports/net/samba4/work/=20
 1. make a backup of the libraries in /usr/local/lib with the same names as =
 the ones in /usr/ports/net/samba4/work/
 2. copy the libraries from /usr/ports/net/samba4/work/ to /usr/local/lib
 note that some libraries are directly located in /usr/local/lib, some in /u=
 sr/local/lib/samba and some are symlinked to other names.=20
 
 The libraries should be located in=20
 /usr/ports/net/samba4/work/samba-4.0.13/bin/shared/private/
 /usr/ports/net/samba4/work/samba-4.0.12/bin/shared/
 
 In short:
 mkdir -p ~/samba_lib_backup/usr/local/lib/samba
 cd /usr/ports/net/samba4/work/samba-4.0.12/bin/shared/
 f=3D`ls`; for i in $f ; do if [ -x "/usr/local/lib/$i" ]; then cp -p /usr/l=
 ocal/lib/$i ~/samba_lib_backup/usr/local/lib/$i ; else echo missing $i ; fi=
 ; done
 cd /usr/ports/net/samba4/work/samba-4.0.12/bin/shared/private/
 f=3D`ls`; for i in $f ; do if [ -x "/usr/local/lib/samba/$i" ]; then cp -p =
 /usr/local/lib/$i ~/samba_lib_backup/usr/local/lib/samba/$i ; else echo mis=
 sing $i ; fi; done
 
 # this should have created a backup of the libraries in your users home
 
 # probably need to do the following as root
 cd /usr/ports/net/samba4/work/samba-4.0.12/bin/shared/
 cp * /usr/local/lib/
 cd /usr/ports/net/samba4/work/samba-4.0.12/bin/shared/private/
 cp * /usr/local/lib/samba/
 
 I then checked that the permissions were the same for the new libraries as =
 the backup i created.=20
 
 Maybe not the correct way of doing it, but it worked for me.=20
 changing the Makefile to have the flags -L/usr/ports/net/samba4/work/samba-=
 4.0.13/bin/shared/private/ -L/usr/ports/net/samba4/work/samba-4.0.12/bin/sh=
 ared/ when linking is probably a better way.
 
 hope it helps, good luck.
 Gustav
 ________________________________________
 Fr=E5n: Andrew Chanler [achanler@gmail.com]
 Skickat: den 17 december 2013 14:13
 Till: bug-followup@FreeBSD.org; Gustav Bergquist
 =C4mne: Re: ports/183285: ports net/samba4 4.0.8 fails to build. Linking er=
 ror
 
 I am hitting this same bug while trying to upgrade from  net/samba4 version=
  4.0.8 to version 4.0.13.  There are lots of errors during linking that say=
  'undefined reference to '...@SAMBA_4.0.8'.
 
 4.0.8 is the first version I built/installed.  This is the first time I tri=
 ed updating this port to a newer version because of three security audit wa=
 rnings from running 'portaudit -Fva'
 
 I was running Free BSD 9.1-RELEASE.  I updated to 9.2-RELEASE and still hav=
 e same link error while building net/samba4 version 4.0.13.
 
 Any known workarounds/fixes to build this port?
 
 Thanks,
 Andrew=
